name: wiki-search-results
description: Ordered list of search hits: article title link + plain-text excerpt (~180 chars). Zero-results state with a prompt to try different terms. Backed by the Tantivy JSON-RPC endpoint at /mcp (see research note).
research_notes: Tantivy search API shape confirmed 2026-05-06 (project-knowledge codebase, search.rs:59-65 + server.rs:309-328).
Response: {query, count, hits: [{slug, title, snippet}]}. No <mark> HTML — plain text only.
Snippet: first non-empty, non-heading paragraph, ~180 chars, truncated at word boundary with '…'.
Endpoint: POST /mcp, JSON-RPC 2.0, method='search', params={q, limit:10} (HTML page uses limit:25).
score field computed internally (BM25) but not returned in JSON.
html:
{{count}} result{{#plural}}s{{/plural}} for "{{query}}"

  1. {{title}}

    {{snippet}}

No results for "{{query}}". Try fewer or different keywords.
aria: <section aria-label> wraps the results. aria-live='polite' + aria-atomic='true' causes SR to announce count when results update. Snippet text: plain text from Tantivy, no HTML manipulation needed. Zero-results div toggled via [hidden] attribute (not display:none) for SR compatibility.
open_questions: Tantivy Phase 3.2+ may add query-aware snippet generation with <mark> highlights. When that ships, the excerpt template will need a sanitised innerHTML assignment instead of textContent.
